consistency.data_completeness: Complete. All expected fields for a standard spell entry are present.
consistency.naming_conventions: Standard and consistent naming conventions appear to be used for keys (e.g., 'casting_time', 'slug').

quality.readability: High. The description is well-formatted, including bolding for the 'At Higher Levels' section.
quality.clarity: Excellent. The effect, range (15-foot cone), damage (3d6), and saving throw (Dexterity) are clearly defined.

mechanics.core_mechanic: Area of Effect (AoE) damaging spell with a Dexterity saving throw for half damage.
mechanics.scaling: Standard scaling mechanic: +1d6 damage per spell slot level above 1st.
mechanics.interaction_notes: Includes a secondary effect: ignition of flammable objects not worn or carried, which is typical for fire spells.

analysis.overall_assessment: High quality, standard entry for the 'Burning Hands' spell. Mechanics are clear and consistent with established rulesets.
